Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies SpLFS as Sopen05g008450 in the Summary, Methods, and Results.

Regulator_Type.reason: SpLFS is described as an AP2/ERF family member/transcription factor throughout the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states SpLFS is an AP2/ERF family TF and that it has a single AP2 DNA-binding domain.

Source_Species.reason: SpLFS was studied in Solanum pennellii.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly given as Solanum pennellii.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The plant material used is S. pennellii LA0716.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays for SpLFS were performed in Solanum pennellii.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species for SpLFS VIGS and reporter work is Solanum pennellii.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The experiments used S. pennellii accession LA0716.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show reduced acylsugars and reduced methylated flavonol identified as dimethyl kaempferol in SpLFS-VIGS plants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are explicitly acylsugars and a flavonoid/methylated flavonol.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Summary states acylsugars provide protection against biotic and abiotic stresses, but this function was not directly tested for SpLFS-regulated products in the Results; flavonoid function is not established here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper states acylsugars and certain flavonoids are in glandular trichomes of aerial tissues, and SpLFS promoter activity is predominantly in tip cells of digitate type I/IV trichomes.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: VIGS of SpLFS reduced total acylsugars by c. 65% and reduced dimethyl kaempferol; the paper concludes SpLFS positively regulates acylsugar biosynthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported by the Results: ~208-fold trichome enrichment, reduced acylsugars/acyl chains/flavonoid upon VIGS, RNA-seq downregulation, WGCNA co-expression, and EMSA binding to target promoters.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The Results and Discussion support SpLFS control of trichome specialized metabolism, including acylsugar and flavonoid pathway gene expression.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: The paper states auxin-inducible relationship is reported for close tomato homologs and distinguishes this from SpLFS, for which it is not established here.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper reports a trichome-development effect, but stronger effects on acylsugars than trichome density, and promoter activity mainly in type I/IV trichome tip cells.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the paper for SpLFS: VIGS, LC-MS, GC-MS, RNA-seq, WGCNA, EMSA, promoter::GFP reporter, and RT-qPCR.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: RNA-seq showed downregulation of many acylsugar/flavonoid genes in SpLFS-VIGS plants, consistent with positive regulation.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These listed items are genes/promoters tested by EMSA or discussed as targets, not proteins shown to physically interact with SpLFS. No protein-protein interactions were reported.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: These promoters/genes are explicitly discussed as direct or candidate direct targets; EMSA showed binding to Sopen02g027670, Sopen09g030520, Sopen06g034830, SpKAR1, SpIPMS3, and SpASAT3 promoters.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion explicitly states the work opens the possibility of metabolic engineering of acylsugar production in solanaceous crops; caution about developmental effects is a reasonable inference from reported roles.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Growth conditions are explicitly reported, and imazapyr treatment is discussed as prior data associated with repression of SpLFS and acylsugar genes rather than a core treatment in this study.

Applied_Promoters.reason: A 1832-bp native SpLFS promoter fragment was used for reporter analysis, and TRV-based VIGS was used for silencing.

Regulation_Mode.reason: EMSA showed direct promoter binding to GCC box/GCC-like motifs and VIGS caused reduced target-gene expression, supporting transcriptional activation/positive regulation.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper concludes SpLFS is a critical positive regulator of acylsugar biosynthesis, and correlation with acylsugar level is stronger than with trichome density.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No direct subcellular localization experiment or explicit statement that SpLFS localizes to the nucleus was reported in this paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR and reporter analyses show ~208-fold trichome enrichment, expression in seedlings and trichome-bearing aerial tissues, little in roots, and predominant promoter activity in type I/IV trichome tip cells.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: For S. pennellii, the paper reports high trichome-enriched expression and strongest promoter activity in type I/IV trichome tip cells on aerial tissues.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data from this study are stated to be available under BioProject PRJNA821975.

corrections.old_value: Sopen02g027670; Sopen09g030520; Sopen06g034830; Sopen05g009610; Sopen08g005140; Sopen11g026960
corrections.reason: These listed items are genes/promoters tested by EMSA or discussed as targets, not proteins shown to physically interact with SpLFS. No protein-protein interactions were reported.
No direct subcellular localization experiment or explicit statement that SpLFS localizes to the nucleus was reported in this paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: These exact N. benthamiana gene IDs are explicitly listed in the VIGS Methods and Results.

Regulator_Type.reason: They are presented as close homologs/orthologs of SpLFS, which is an AP2/ERF transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies them as SpLFS homologs/orthologs within the AP2/ERF family context.

Source_Species.reason: These genes are from Nicotiana benthamiana.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species.reason: VIGS testing of these orthologs was performed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is explicitly Nicotiana benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The measured output in N. benthamiana VIGS experiments was acylsugar production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The final product measured is acylsugars.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper generally states acylsugars protect against biotic and abiotic stresses, but this function was not directly tested for these N. benthamiana orthologs in the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Acylsugars were measured from leaf exudates/leaf surface, but the specific phrase 'leaf surface glandular trichomes' for N. benthamiana was not directly demonstrated in the Results of this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results state VIGS of the two genes caused c. 15% overall reduction in acylsugar production, with 3–30% reduction in individual acylsugars.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The Results support selection of two upper-clade orthologs for testing, reduced acylsugar accumulation after VIGS, and interpretation as conserved acylsugar-biosynthesis function.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper concludes the orthologs are involved in acylsugar biosynthesis.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The Discussion explicitly proposes smaller effect size may reflect incomplete silencing or redundancy from Niben101Scf03045g14001.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: N. benthamiana orthologs were examined using VIGS, LC-MS metabolite analysis, and phylogenetic analysis.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper does not explicitly discuss breeding/engineering applications for the N. benthamiana orthologs specifically; this is extrapolative.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown under the controlled conditions described in Plant materials and growth conditions, and TRV-VIGS was the treatment used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: TRV-based VIGS was used to target these genes in N. benthamiana.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Positive effect on acylsugar accumulation is supported by VIGS phenotype, but direct transcriptional regulation was not shown for these specific N. benthamiana genes.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: They contribute positively to acylsugar accumulation, but the modest effect and possible redundancy make 'decisive' stronger than what the paper directly shows.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No localization data for these N. benthamiana proteins were presented.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper says all three close homologs showed similar RNA-seq coverage in Bombarely et al. data and that the tested genes are in the upper clade; however, it does not directly show these exact N. benthamiana genes are trichome-enriched.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly cites Bombarely et al. (2012) expression data showing similar RNA-seq coverage for the three close homologs, while not providing a separate accession in this paper.

corrections.reason: No localization data for these N. benthamiana proteins were presented.
